summary: DOM debugging investigation of Concierge® floating button on /celebrate-soul (1440x900). Used document.elementFromPoint, fixed-element scans, DOM chain analysis, JS click test, and Playwright mouse click to identify the intercepting element. RESULT: Button IS clickable in headless Playwright; modal opens correctly. No intercepting overlay found.

ui_bugs.issue: Button has NO data-testid attribute. Review request expected data-testid='concierge-builder-float-btn' but it does not exist in DOM. This is a missing testability attribute.

Pill-shape (border-radius:999px) creates dead zones at rounded corners. At x>1400 or x=1217, and near top/bottom edges (y≈757 or y≈800), elementFromPoint returns MAIN content instead of the button. For example: (1400,757) → MAIN, (1410,800) → MAIN. User clicking the visual corner area will miss the button. The button center (1316,780) works correctly.

ui_bugs.component: MiraChatWidget - MiraOrb container vs Concierge button boundary
ui_bugs.issue: The Concierge button bottom edge (y=804) and MiraOrb container top edge (y=804) share an exact pixel boundary. Sub-pixel rendering differences or DPI scaling in real browsers could cause 1-2px 'dead zone' or orb activation. The MiraOrb outer glow (100px, pointer-events:none) visually overlaps the button from y=778 to y=804, but does NOT intercept clicks due to pointer-events:none.

action_items: INVESTIGATION RESULT: No intercepting element found over the Concierge button in headless Chrome at 1440x900. Button is clickable; modal opens correctly. The user-reported issue could NOT be reproduced in automated testing.
Add data-testid='concierge-builder-float-btn' to the floating Concierge button in PillarPageLayout.jsx lines 468-481 for testability.

Consider adding a slightly larger hit-area to the pill button OR adjusting MiraOrb position so it's not pixel-adjacent to the Concierge button (e.g., move MiraOrb to bottom:24px→bottom:16px or Concierge button to bottom:100px to create gap between them).
If issue persists in real Chrome (not headless), check if browser zoom level or DPI scaling affects the y=804 boundary. Test at 110%, 125% zoom.

Consider testing if the issue occurs specifically AFTER opening/closing the Mira chat widget (body scroll lock useEffect) -- the body is clean in our test but a race condition with cleanup might exist.
critical_code_review_comments: PillarPageLayout.jsx line 469: Concierge button missing data-testid='concierge-builder-float-btn'

MiraChatWidget.jsx lines 584-611: Body scroll lock sets document.body.style.position='fixed'. If cleanup fails (unmount or exception), body stays locked. Consider using a ref to track scroll position instead of body.style manipulation.
MiraOrb.jsx: outerGlow (100px) extends 26px above and below the orb's bounding box. While pointer-events:none prevents click interception, this creates visual overlap with adjacent elements.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Thorough DOM investigation of the Concierge floating button intercept issue on /celebrate-soul at 1440x900. Summary: button works fine in headless Chrome Playwright, modal opens on click. Key data: button rect={x:1217, y:757, w:199, h:47, z:2147483640}, MiraOrb rect={x:1365, y:804, w:51, h:72, z:9999}. All MiraOrb glow elements have pointer-events:none. No transparent overlays. DOM chain at button center: BUTTON → BODY (portal). The only fixed elements on the page are: (1) Concierge button z=2147483640, (2) MiraOrb container z=9999, (3) Emergent badge z=9999 pe=none, (4) Toast OL z=100 pe=none. If user still reports issue in real browser, investigate: browser zoom/DPI scaling, and the exact y=804 pixel boundary between Concierge button and MiraOrb.

rca_of_the_issue.bug_summary: Cannot reproduce click interception in headless Playwright at 1440x900. Button IS clickable and modal opens.
reproduction_steps: Click the '✦ Concierge® Requests' button at approximately (1316, 780)

dom_state.only_interceptor_candidates: None with pointer-events that could block

potential_causes: User clicking on rounded-corner dead zones of pill button (border-radius:999px reduces actual hit area)
Sub-pixel browser rendering difference causing MiraOrb to extend 1-2px upward past y=804 in real Chrome
Browser zoom level (110%+) shifting the pixel-perfect y=804 boundary
Issue may have been present in older code and is now fixed
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Add explicit data-testid to button, add 4-8px vertical gap between Concierge button and MiraOrb by adjusting bottom values, consider using pointerEvents:'all' with an expanded clickable area
